commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e1ee393b0f504d9efe7e6d4f77567da74d2e4fe8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes duplicated OpenAI Codex OAuth browser/token flow from core compatibility helpers and delegates login/refresh through the bundled OpenAI provider runtime while adding focused tests.

PR surface: Source -655, Tests -470. Total -1125 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: no. live reproduction was run. Source comparison is enough to reproduce the compatibility concern: current main runs the helper directly, while PR head throws when the provider runtime OAuth hook is missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human auth/provider review is required because the blocker is a compatibility and upgrade-policy decision on a protected maintainer PR, not a narrow automation repair.

target.title: refactor(openai): centralize Codex OAuth flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Refactor OpenAI Codex OAuth to remove duplicated browser/token flow code; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
- Keep the existing OpenAI plugin OAuth runtime as the single implementation used by both plugin auth and legacy compatibility wrappers.
- Preserve legacy `src/llm` login/refresh behavior, including abort propagation, immediate manual-code input, callback handling, and credential refresh shape.
- Provenance: duplicated OpenAI Codex OAuth flow was introduced by `bb46b79d3c14` (`refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime`, #85341).


## Compatibility Review

- Deprecated `src/llm` OpenAI Codex OAuth login/refresh entry points are preserved and covered by focused compatibility tests. The login wrapper still reports browser auth through `onAuth`, still routes fallback prompts through `onPrompt`, still starts `onManualCodeInput` in parallel with browser auth when supplied, and now passes the caller `signal` into the provider-owned OAuth hook.
- No plugin SDK `ProviderAuthContext` fields are added. Legacy-only cancellation and manual-code hooks are carried through a private OpenAI Codex bridge context, so external provider auth consumers do not get a new API surface.
- Current-main token behavior remains plugin-owned: bounded token request timeout, SSRF-guarded fetch, cancellation, TLS/proxy preflight, callback handling, and error rewriting stay in `extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth*.runtime.ts` instead of being copied back into core.
- Live OpenAI browser OAuth with real credentials was not run and is not required for this refactor before maintainer review: this PR removes duplicate implementation and delegates to the existing plugin runtime, while focused tests/build/lint/autoreview cover the changed compatibility and delegation surfaces. A live credential run can still be requested separately if maintainers want release-style auth proof.


## Live Fix Proof Repro Matrix

Remote proof was run through Crabbox using delegated Blacksmith Testbox.

- Provider: `blacksmith-testbox` via Crabbox
- Testbox id: `tbx_01kspb16xfdpbqvtpsepp2fpzf`
- Actions run: `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26553425361`
- Result: exit 0

Matrix covered:

| Phase | Proof |
|---|---|
| `oauth-runtime` | `extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts` passed: callback, manual-code, token exchange/refresh, timeout, cancellation coverage |
| `openai-provider` | `extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts` and `extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts` passed |
| `legacy-compat` | `src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts` passed: legacy callback routing, immediate manual-code input, cancellation propagation, refresh shape |
| `provider-bridge` | `src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts` passed: bridge delegates to OpenAI provider auth hook and returns OAuth credential/null correctly |
| `refresh-fallback` | `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.openai-codex-refresh-fallback.test.ts` passed in both agents-core and agents-support projects |
| `lint` | Focused `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` and `git diff --check` passed |
| `build` | `pnpm build` passed on remote Linux/Testbox |

True credentialed OpenAI browser OAuth was not run because it requires a real OpenAI account/session and secret-safe human/browser interaction. The refactor proof above exercises the happy paths reachable without real credentials and verifies the changed delegation/cancellation/manual-input surfaces on remote Linux.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.openai-codex-refresh-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.ts src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth.runtime.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/types.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Additional focused validation after narrowing the bridge to avoid exported `ProviderAuthContext` changes:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/types.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation duplication between the bundled OpenAI plugin and legacy core OAuth helpers; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
Real environment tested: Local macOS worktree with installed repo dependencies.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final code changes.
Evidence after fix: Focused OpenAI Codex OAuth, provider auth, compatibility, refresh fallback tests passed locally and in Crabbox/Testbox remote proof; focused oxlint and `git diff --check` passed; `pnpm build` passed locally and remotely; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: Legacy compatibility surfaces now delegate to the existing provider-owned OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation while preserving cancellation and parallel manual-code input behavior; the duplicated core browser/token implementation is removed without adding exported provider-auth context fields.
What was not tested: Live OpenAI browser OAuth against real OpenAI credentials was not run because it requires a real OpenAI account/session and secret-safe human/browser interaction.
target.author: romneyda
